name: Pan-Fried Noodles
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Bok choy, cut into 2-in sections
1 tbsp Cornstarch dissolved in 2 tbsp Cold chicken broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch 1 pound fresh noodles in boiling water to wash away starch and loosen them (about 2 minutes). Drain well.
2. Scatter noodles in a baking pan. Untangle with chopsticks and hands.
3. Place a layer of noodles in each of two large, heavy skillets.
4. Add 2 tablespoons oil and 1/3 cup chicken broth to each skillet.
5. Cook over low flame until broth boils away and bottom browns and crisps (about 10-12 minutes).
6. Occasionally add oil and broth to keep noodles moist and allow crust to develop slowly.
7. When ready, shake pan to ensure cake isn't stuck; if it is, dribble oil around edge to loosen.
8. Flip skillet over with a lid, holding firmly. Cake will drop onto lid.
9. Slide cake back into pan to brown on other side. Add more oil if needed.
10. Serve on plate and use as base for dishes with sauce.
To make the Pork and Vegetable Sauce:
1. Heat peanut oil in a wok.
2. Add bok choy and stir-fry until coated in oil.
3. Add pork pieces and heat through.
4. Stir in broth, soy sauce, rice wine/sherry, and oyster sauce.
5. Thicken sauce with cornstarch when hot. Serve over pan-fried noodles.
